title: Is the IMAT 2026 syllabus confirmed yet?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Emma. I've been searching the official channels but can't find anything definitive about the 2026 syllabus. Do you think it will change much from last year?
author: Emma

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: Usually, the changes are very minor. I wouldn't worry too much yet, stick to the current one for now.

answers.author: Lukas
answers.content: I agree with Matteo. They might tweak some details in biology or general knowledge, but the core remains the same.

answers.author: Giulia
answers.content: Wait, I heard there might be a bigger change in the logic section? Has anyone else heard that?

answers.author: Finn
answers.content: I haven't seen anything official about the logic section. It's probably just rumors!

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: Actually, last year they changed the number of questions in some sections. So keep an eye out for that!

answers.author: Zeynep
answers.content: Good point Sofia. I'm focusing on the sciences first, as those are usually stable.

answers.author: Adel
answers.content: Yeah, sciences are the safe bet. Anyone have a link to the 2025 decree for reference?

answers.author: Bence
answers.content: Here is the link: [Universitaly IMAT 2025]. It's the best reference we have right now.

answers.author: Emma
answers.content: Thanks Bence! This is super helpful. I'll stick to the 2025 one for now then.

answers.author: Luca
answers.content: Glad we cleared that up. This thread is full of good info!

title: Struggling with organic chemistry nomenclature
content: Hey! I'm Liam. I'm find the organic chemistry part of the IMAT really hard. Especially naming the more complex molecules. Any tips or shortcuts?
author: Liam

answers.author: Jacob
answers.content: Try learning the functional groups in order of priority. That's the most important part of nomenclature.

answers.author: Lina
answers.content: I found a really good YouTube channel for this. Search for 'Organic Chemistry Tutor', his videos are amazing!

answers.author: Chiara
answers.content: Also, practice drawing the molecules yourself. It helps to visualize the structure and the name.

answers.author: Mert
answers.content: Naming is just about following the IUPAC rules. Once you get the basics, it becomes much easier.

title: IMAT preparation from a non-science background?
content: Hi, I'm Ava. I'm a humanities student but I've decided to apply for medicine in Italy. Is it possible to prepare well for the IMAT without a strong science background?
author: Ava

answers.author: Emre
answers.content: It's definitely possible, but it will take more time and effort. You'll need to catch up on all the basic concepts first.

answers.author: Mia
answers.content: I was in a similar situation! I started with basic textbooks and then moved to more specific prep books. It was hard but manageable.

title: Anyone applying for Milan IMS next year?
content: Hey guys, I'm Noah. Just wondering who else is aiming for the International Medical School in Milan for 2026. It's a very competitive place!
author: Noah

title: IMAT: How important is General Knowledge?
content: Hello everyone, I'm Hanna. I'm find the general knowledge section so random. Is it worth spending many hours on it or should I focus on something more predictable?
author: Hanna

answers.author: Alessandro
answers.content: Focus on the sciences first! GK is too unpredictable. Just keep up with some major news and historical events.

title: IMAT Physics: Mechanics vs Electromagnetism
content: Hi, I'm Elif. For the physics part of the IMAT, which section is usually more frequent? Mechanics or electromagnetism? I'm better at mechanics...
author: Elif

answers.author: Lukas
answers.content: Both are quite common. I'd say mechanics usually has a few more questions, but don't ignore electromagnetism!

answers.author: Finn
answers.content: Mechanics is definitely a big topic. Also, don't forget about thermodynamics, it shows up too!

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: I agree. Focus on your strengths but try to have a basic understanding of everything.

title: IMAT: Best way to handle the time limit?
content: Hello, I'm Bence. 100 minutes for 60 questions is tight. What's your strategy for the actual exam day? Do you skip hard questions and come back later?
author: Bence

answers.author: Giulia
answers.content: Yes, always skip! Every second is precious. Do the easy ones first to build confidence and then tackle the harder ones.

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: Exactly. I usually start with biology as it's the fastest for me. Then chemistry, then physics/math and logic last.

title: Anyone applying for Rome Sapienza next year?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Zalan. Rome is my first choice for medical school. Anyone else here aiming for La Sapienza? It's such a beautiful city!
author: Zalan

title: IMAT Biology: Transcription and Translation
content: Hey! I'm Madison. I'm currently studying molecular biology. For the IMAT, do we need to know all the specific enzymes involved in transcription and translation?
author: Madison

answers.author: Olivia
answers.content: Yes, focus on the major ones like RNA polymerase and the ribosomal units. Knowing the overall process is also very important.

title: IMAT: Is the math section very advanced?
content: Hi guys, I'm Liam. I haven't done much math since high school. Is the level of math required for the IMAT very hard? Should I be worried about calculus?
author: Liam

answers.author: Jacob
answers.content: No calculus! It's mostly algebra, functions, geometry, and basic trigonometry. Nothing too advanced usually.

title: IMAT results: How long to get the scores?
content: Hello everyone, I'm Emma. For those who recently took the IMAT, how long after the exam day did it take to get your preliminary scores? Was it a long wait?
author: Emma

answers.author: Noah
answers.content: It usually takes about 2-3 weeks for the preliminary results and then a few more for the final ranking. And yes, it's very stressful!

title: IMAT study group in Milan?
content: Hi! I'm Sofia. I just moved to Milan to start my preparation for the IMAT. Is anyone else here based in Milan and interested in starting a study group?
author: Sofia

title: IMAT: Can I use a calculator?
content: Hey guys, I'm Mert. Just a quick question: are we allowed to use calculators during the IMAT? My mental math is definitely not my strongest skill...
author: Mert

answers.author: Lina
answers.content: Unfortunately, no! You'll need to rely on your mental math skills. Better start practicing now!

title: Anyone applying for Pavia IMS next year?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Zeynep. I'm very interested in the International Medical School in Pavia. Has anyone else here applying there? Would love to connect!
author: Zeynep

answers.author: Chiara
answers.content: Pavia is a great place to study! The medical school is very prestigious and the student life is really nice.

title: IMAT: Dealing with exam anxiety
content: Hello, I'm Hanna. The thought of the IMAT is making me really anxious. How do you guys cope with the pressure? Any tips or relaxation techniques?
author: Hanna

answers.author: Emma
answers.content: Try some deep breathing exercises or meditation. Also, make sure to take regular breaks and get enough sleep during your preparation.

title: IMAT Biology: Genetics problems
content: Hi guys, I'm Jacob. I'm struggling with those genetics problems where you have to calculate probabilities for different traits. Any good resources for this?
author: Jacob

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: Khan Academy has some really good videos on Punnett squares and genetics. Definitely check them out, they are very clear!

title: Anyone applying for Bologna IMS next year?
content: Hey everyone, I'm Mia. Bologna is my first choice for medical school. Anyone else here aiming for the international course there? It's such a vibrant city!
author: Mia

title: IMAT Physics: Thermodynamics laws
content: Hi, I'm Lukas. I'm currently reviewing thermodynamics. How much of this is usually on the IMAT? Is it just the basic laws or something more detailed?
author: Lukas

answers.author: Finn
answers.content: Mostly the three laws and basic heat transfer concepts. Not too deep usually, but you should know the main formulas.

title: IMAT: Guessing strategy?
content: Hello everyone, I'm Luca. With the negative marking, what's your strategy for guessing? Do you only answer if you are 100% sure, or do you take risks?
author: Luca

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: I only guess if I can eliminate at least two options. Otherwise, the risk of losing 0.4 points is too high for me.

title: IMAT Chemistry: Equilibrium and Le Chatelier
content: Hey! I'm Leon. I'm currently studying chemical equilibrium. Any common questions or tips about Le Chatelier's principle for the IMAT?
author: Leon

answers.author: Adel
answers.content: Yes, Le Chatelier is very common! Make sure you understand how changes in pressure, temperature, and concentration affect the equilibrium.

title: IMAT: Which section is the hardest?
content: Hi everyone, I'm Bence. In your experience, which section of the IMAT is usually the hardest? For me, it's definitely the logic and critical thinking.
author: Bence

answers.author: Giulia
answers.content: For me, it's chemistry! There's so much to memorize and the problems can be quite tricky. Logic is actually fun for me!

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: I agree with Giulia about chemistry. Logic just takes practice to understand the common patterns.

answers.author: Emre
answers.content: Bio is the most content-heavy for sure. Understanding the concepts is key, not just memorizing everything.

answers.author: Emma
answers.content: I find the math/physics part hardest because I'm not a 'numbers' person. But I'm practicing a lot!

answers.author: Lukas
answers.content: Everyone has their own strengths and weaknesses. The important thing is to focus on what you find hardest.

answers.author: Zeynep
answers.content: Exactly! This thread just shows how everyone is different. Good luck to all with your weakest sections!

title: IMAT prep: How many past papers are enough?
content: Hello everyone, I'm Zalan. I've finished most of the past papers from 2011 to 2024. Is that enough, or should I look for other similar tests like BMAT or SAT?
author: Zalan

title: IMAT: Can I skip the entire Physics section?
content: Hi guys, I'm Ashley. I'm really struggling with physics and it's only 4 questions. Is it a big risk if I just skip it and focus on getting all the biology and chemistry right?
author: Ashley

answers.author: Jacob
answers.content: It's a common strategy for some. But remember, every point counts! Maybe just try to learn the most frequent topics in physics.

title: IMAT results: Preliminary vs Final ranking
content: Hi everyone, I'm Emma. What's the main difference between the preliminary scores and the final ranking? Does the ranking change much after they review everything?
author: Emma

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: The preliminary score is just your points. The final ranking includes things like priority, scrolling, and quota. It can definitely change the outcome!

title: Applying as a non-EU student living in Italy?
content: Hey! I'm Zeynep. I'm a non-EU citizen but I've been living in Italy for a few years. Do I apply through the non-EU or EU quota for medical school?
author: Zeynep

answers.author: Alessandro
answers.content: If you have a long-term residency permit, you might be eligible for the EU quota. Check with the university's admission office for your specific case.

title: IMAT: How important is General Knowledge?
content: Hello everyone, I'm Hanna. I've heard some people say that general knowledge is just easy points, and others say it's impossible to study for. What's your take on it?
author: Hanna

title: IMAT Chemistry: Acids, Bases, and pH
content: Hi, I'm Lina. I'm currently reviewing acids and bases. For the IMAT, do we need to calculate pH for weak acids as well, or is it mostly strong ones?
author: Lina

answers.author: Luca
answers.content: Mostly strong acids and bases. For weak ones, you might just need to understand the concept of Ka and Kb.

title: Anyone applying for Turin IMS next year?
content: Hey everyone, I'm Sofia. Turin is one of my choices for medical school. Is anyone else here planning to apply for the international course there in 2026?
author: Sofia

answers.author: Lucia
answers.content: I'm considering Turin too! It seems like a great city with a lot of clinical opportunities.

title: IMAT: Dealing with time pressure
content: Hello, I'm Bence. During my timed practice tests, I always feel rushed at the end. How do you guys manage your pace during the actual exam? Any specific milestones?
author: Bence

answers.author: Sofia
answers.content: I try to keep an eye on the time every 10 minutes. For example, I aim to finish the first 25 questions within the first 40 minutes.

title: IMAT Biology: Cell structure and organelles
content: Hi guys, I'm Elif. For the cell section, do we need to know all the specific functions of every single organelle? Things like peroxisomes or the cytoskeleton?
author: Elif

answers.author: Matteo
answers.content: Yes, they can definitely ask about those! Focus on the main ones like mitochondria and ribosomes, but have a basic idea of all organelles.

title: Anyone from the US studying in Italy?
content: Hey! I'm Jacob. I'm really curious about the transition from the US education system to the Italian one. Is it a big adjustment for students from the US?
author: Jacob

title: IMAT: Best way to review science topics?
content: Hello everyone, I'm Hanna. I'm looking for the most efficient way to review all the science topics before the final month. Any specific methods or apps?
author: Hanna

answers.author: Emma
answers.content: I find that doing a lot of practice questions and then reviewing the topics where I get things wrong is the most effective way for me.

title: IMAT Chemistry: Molarity and Molality
content: Hi guys, I'm Mert. Can someone explain the main difference between molarity and molality? I always get them mixed up when solving problems...
author: Mert

answers.author: Lukas
answers.content: Molarity is moles of solute per liter of SOLUTION. Molality is moles of solute per kilogram of SOLVENT. Remember: molality is 'mass'!

title: Anyone applying for Rome Sapienza (Med-School)?
content: Hey! I'm Zalan. Rome is my top choice for medicine. Is anyone else here applying for the international course at La Sapienza? How are you preparing?
author: Zalan

title: IMAT Physics: Circuits and electricity
content: Hi, I'm Noah. I'm currently studying DC circuits. Is electricity a common topic in the IMAT physics section? Should I spend much time on it?
author: Noah

answers.author: Emre
answers.content: Yes, electricity is quite common! Make sure you understand resistors in series and parallel, and Ohm's law well.
